name: Consult No-Show Killer

parameters.content:
## Consult No-Show Killer

### How it works
Receives every new booking from Calendly or any scheduling tool. Validates that the event is a new booking (not a cancellation), builds all four message texts, and calculates the exact send times. Before each SMS, the Bar-Compliance Guardrail checks the opt-out list and appends the TCPA disclaimer. Sends an instant confirmation by SMS and email — then waits and sends a 24-hour reminder, a 1-hour reminder, and finally a gentle reschedule nudge 15 minutes after the scheduled start.

### Setup steps
1. **n8n Variables** (project → Variables tab): `FIRM_NAME`, `FIRM_EMAIL`, `FIRM_FROM_EMAIL`, `FIRM_TWILIO_NUMBER`, `FIRM_TIMEZONE` (e.g. `America/New_York`), `FIRM_BOOKING_URL`, `GUARDRAIL_WORKFLOW_ID` (numeric ID from the Bar-Compliance Guardrail workflow URL)
2. **Credentials**: wire `Twilio account` and `Email account (SMTP)` in project → Credentials tab
3. **Booking tool webhook**: paste the Production URL from When Booking Received into your scheduling tool — select new-booking events. Calendly requires API registration; see the deploy guide for the exact `curl` command.
4. **Bar-Compliance Guardrail**: deploy it first and paste its workflow ID into `GUARDRAIL_WORKFLOW_ID`
5. **Database** (self-hosted only): must use Postgres — not SQLite — so long waits survive restarts. n8n Cloud handles this automatically.

### Customization
- **Message text**: edit the four SMS strings in Set Up Messages
- **Timing**: adjust `MS_24H`, `MS_1H`, `MS_15M` constants in Set Up Messages
- **Cancellations**: stop the running execution manually in n8n → Executions (automatic cancellation detection is a paid upgrade)

**Replaces:** Lawmatics reminder sequences ($0–$149/mo) · Paid Calendly reminder tiers ($12/seat/mo)

**Receive and validate booking**
Accepts new bookings from Calendly or any scheduling tool. Normalises the payload and filters out cancellations and rescheduled events before any messages are sent.

**Reject invalid bookings**
Cancellations, reschedule confirmations, and events missing contact details all exit here silently — no messages sent.

**Set up messages**
Builds all four message texts and calculates the exact fire time for each reminder from n8n Variables.

**Confirmation**
Compliance check → if approved, sends confirmation SMS and email. If opted out, suppression is logged and the reminder sequence is stopped.

**24-hour reminder**
Workflow pauses until 24 hours before the appointment, then runs a compliance check and sends the 24h reminder by SMS and email.

**1-hour reminder**
Workflow pauses until 1 hour before the appointment, then runs a compliance check and sends the final pre-consult reminder with the reschedule link.

**No-show follow-up**
Workflow pauses until 15 minutes after the scheduled start, then runs a compliance check and sends a neutral reschedule nudge by SMS and email.

nodes.name: When Booking Received
nodes.notes:
Receives the booking event from your scheduling tool.

This URL is the address your booking tool will notify whenever someone books a consult. Copy the Production URL from this node (visible after you activate the workflow) and register it with your booking tool.

Calendly: Calendly v2 requires registering the webhook via API — you cannot paste a URL through the Calendly interface. See the deploy guide for the exact registration command. Activate the workflow first so the URL is live before registering.

Other tools (Acuity, Cal.com, Fluent Forms, practice management systems): paste the Production URL into your tool's webhook or notification settings and select the 'new booking created' event. The exact label varies by tool.

The next step (Normalize and Validate Booking) automatically detects whether the event came from Calendly or another tool and pulls the right fields from each.

nodes.name: Normalize and Validate Booking
nodes.notes:
Reads the incoming booking event and extracts everything the workflow needs: the prospect's name, email, phone number, appointment time, consult type, and whether this is a new booking or a cancellation.

This step works with both Calendly and other booking tools — it detects which one sent the event and pulls the right fields from each.

Phone number for Calendly: the workflow looks first for Calendly's built-in text reminder number field, then for any question-and-answer field whose label contains the word 'phone'. If neither is present, the SMS steps will skip gracefully and email still sends.

Validity check: only new bookings proceed. Cancellations and reschedule confirmations are filtered out at the next step so no reminders are sent for a booking that has already been cancelled.

Output available to all later steps: contact_name, contact_email, contact_phone, appointment_time, consult_type, reschedule_url, booking_event, source, is_valid_booking, timestamp.

nodes.name: If Valid Booking
nodes.notes:
Decides whether to proceed or stop — only genuine new bookings trigger the reminder sequence.

Calendly sends notifications for cancellations and reschedule confirmations on the same webhook endpoint. Without this check, a cancelled booking could kick off a confirmation message and a full reminder sequence for an appointment that will never happen.

Yes (new booking with contact details and appointment time) → proceed to opt-out check and messages.
No (cancellation, reschedule confirmation, or missing contact details) → stop here, nothing sent.

nodes.name: Ignore Invalid Booking
nodes.notes: Safely ends the workflow for cancellations, reschedule confirmations, and events with missing contact details. No messages are sent. This endpoint is visible in the execution history so you can confirm the filter is working correctly.

nodes.name: Set Up Messages
nodes.notes:
Writes all four message texts and calculates the exact times each reminder should fire.

Reads from n8n Variables (project → Variables tab):
  FIRM_NAME — your law firm's name as it should appear in messages
  FIRM_EMAIL — your intake email address (used for firm-side copies of reminders)
  FIRM_FROM_EMAIL — the email address reminder emails are sent from; must match an address your SMTP account is authorised to send from
  FIRM_TWILIO_NUMBER — your Twilio number in +1XXXXXXXXXX format
  FIRM_BOOKING_URL — your scheduling link, used as the reschedule link if your booking tool doesn't include one per booking
  FIRM_TIMEZONE — your state's timezone (e.g. 'America/Chicago', 'America/Los_Angeles')

All four text messages are personalised with the prospect's first name when available. All include 'Reply STOP to opt out' as required by TCPA law.

Timing notes: if a booking comes in less than 24 hours before the appointment (same-day booking), the 24-hour reminder fires within 90 seconds of confirmation. The reschedule nudge always fires 15 minutes after the scheduled start regardless of whether the prospect attended.

All later steps in this workflow read their data from this node — not from their own immediate input — because Twilio and email responses have a different shape than the booking data.

nodes.name: Compliance Check — Confirmation Text
nodes.notes:
Calls the Bar-Compliance Guardrail before sending the SMS.

Checks whether the client has opted out. If not, confirms the TCPA opt-out instruction is present in the message and logs the send attempt to your audit sheet.

The guardrail workflow ID is read from the GUARDRAIL_WORKFLOW_ID n8n Variable (project → Variables tab). Set it to the numeric ID from the Bar-Compliance Guardrail workflow URL: .../workflow/WORKFLOW_ID.

Returns: { approved, message_out, recipient, suppression_reason } plus all upstream fields.

nodes.name: If Approved to Send Confirmation
nodes.notes:
Routes based on the compliance guardrail's decision.

Yes (approved) → sends the SMS.
No (opted out) → message suppressed; guardrail has logged the suppression.

nodes.name: Skip — opted out (confirmation)
nodes.notes: Client is on the opt-out list — confirmation text not sent and the full reminder sequence is stopped. The Bar-Compliance Guardrail has logged the suppression.

nodes.name: Send Confirmation Text
nodes.notes:
Sends the instant booking confirmation text to the prospect — so they know the appointment is locked in before they even close the booking page.

Credential to wire: In n8n go to project → Credentials tab → New, search for Twilio, and enter your Account SID and Auth Token from the Twilio Console. Save it as 'Twilio account'.

If the prospect did not provide a phone number, Twilio will return an error. The workflow handles this gracefully — it continues to the confirmation email rather than stopping. Both the text and the email are always attempted.

nodes.name: Send Confirmation Email
nodes.notes:
Sends the booking confirmation email to the prospect.

Credential to wire: In n8n go to project → Credentials tab → New, search for SMTP, and enter your email provider's settings. This works with Gmail, Outlook 365, Zoho Mail, or any SMTP-capable email service. Save it as 'Email account (SMTP)'.

The FROM_EMAIL you set in Set Up Messages must be an address your SMTP account is authorised to send from. For most firms this will be the same as FIRM_EMAIL (e.g. intake@smithlaw.com).

All contact and firm details are read from the Set Up Messages node — not from the immediate input — because the Twilio response (from the previous step) has a different data shape than the booking details.

parameters.subject: =Your {{ $('Set Up Messages').item.json.consult_type }} with {{ $('Set Up Messages').item.json.firm_name }} is Confirmed

nodes.name: Wait — 24h Reminder
nodes.notes:
Pauses the workflow until 24 hours before the appointment, then automatically continues to send the reminder.

The workflow execution sits suspended in your n8n database during this wait — it does not consume server resources. When the scheduled time arrives, n8n wakes it up and continues from this point.

For same-day bookings (less than 24 hours until the appointment), this wait fires within 90 seconds so the '24-hour reminder' effectively acts as a second confirmation.

Important: self-hosted n8n must use Postgres as its database for these long waits to survive a restart. n8n Cloud handles this automatically. If you are self-hosted and see executions disappearing after restarts, check your database configuration.

nodes.name: Compliance Check — 24h Reminder
nodes.notes:
Calls the Bar-Compliance Guardrail before sending the SMS.

Checks whether the client has opted out. If not, confirms the TCPA opt-out instruction is present in the message and logs the send attempt to your audit sheet.

The guardrail workflow ID is read from the GUARDRAIL_WORKFLOW_ID n8n Variable (project → Variables tab). Set it to the numeric ID from the Bar-Compliance Guardrail workflow URL: .../workflow/WORKFLOW_ID.

Returns: { approved, message_out, recipient, suppression_reason } plus all upstream fields.

nodes.name: If Approved for 24h Reminder
nodes.notes:
Routes based on the compliance guardrail's decision.

Yes (approved) → sends the SMS.
No (opted out) → message suppressed; guardrail has logged the suppression.

nodes.name: Skip — opted out (24h reminder)
nodes.notes: Client has opted out — 24h reminder suppressed and the remaining reminders are stopped.

nodes.name: Send 24h Reminder Text
nodes.notes:
Sends the 24-hour reminder text to the prospect — the single most effective touchpoint for reducing no-shows.

If the text fails (e.g. the prospect's number is invalid), the workflow continues to the 24-hour reminder email rather than stopping.

All message data is read from the Set Up Messages node — not from the immediate input — because the Wait node's resume data has a different shape than the booking details.

nodes.name: Send 24h Reminder Email
nodes.notes:
Sends the 24-hour reminder email to the prospect.

All contact and firm details are read from the Set Up Messages node — not from the immediate input — because the Twilio response (from the previous step) has a different data shape than the booking details.

parameters.subject: =Reminder: Your {{ $('Set Up Messages').item.json.consult_type }} with {{ $('Set Up Messages').item.json.firm_name }} is Tomorrow

nodes.name: Wait — 1h Reminder
nodes.notes:
Pauses the workflow until 1 hour before the appointment, then continues to send the final pre-consult reminder.

For bookings made less than 1 hour before the appointment, this fires within 3 minutes of the previous step so the prospect still gets a final nudge.

nodes.name: Compliance Check — 1h Reminder
nodes.notes:
Calls the Bar-Compliance Guardrail before sending the SMS.

Checks whether the client has opted out. If not, confirms the TCPA opt-out instruction is present in the message and logs the send attempt to your audit sheet.

The guardrail workflow ID is read from the GUARDRAIL_WORKFLOW_ID n8n Variable (project → Variables tab). Set it to the numeric ID from the Bar-Compliance Guardrail workflow URL: .../workflow/WORKFLOW_ID.

Returns: { approved, message_out, recipient, suppression_reason } plus all upstream fields.

nodes.name: If Approved for 1h Reminder
nodes.notes:
Routes based on the compliance guardrail's decision.

Yes (approved) → sends the SMS.
No (opted out) → message suppressed; guardrail has logged the suppression.

nodes.name: Skip — opted out (1h reminder)
nodes.notes: Client has opted out — 1h reminder suppressed and the no-show check is stopped.

nodes.name: Send 1h Reminder Text
nodes.notes:
Sends the 1-hour reminder text. This message includes the reschedule link so the prospect has a clear, easy option if they can't make it — reducing last-minute no-shows where the prospect simply doesn't show up because they didn't know how to cancel.

If the text fails, the workflow continues to the 1-hour reminder email rather than stopping.

nodes.name: Send 1h Reminder Email
nodes.notes: Sends the 1-hour reminder email with the reschedule link. All contact and firm details are read from the Set Up Messages node.

parameters.subject: =Your {{ $('Set Up Messages').item.json.consult_type }} with {{ $('Set Up Messages').item.json.firm_name }} Starts in 1 Hour

nodes.name: Wait — No-Show Check
nodes.notes:
Pauses the workflow until 15 minutes after the appointment was scheduled to start, then sends the reschedule nudge.

This step fires for all bookings — the free template does not connect to your practice management system to check whether the prospect actually attended. The message is written neutrally ('if anything came up') rather than assuming a no-show, so prospects who did attend are not alienated by an accusatory message.

The done-for-you upgrade adds Clio or MyCase integration to check attendance status before this step, and only sends the reschedule message for genuine no-shows.

nodes.name: Compliance Check — No-Show Text
nodes.notes:
Calls the Bar-Compliance Guardrail before sending the SMS.

Checks whether the client has opted out. If not, confirms the TCPA opt-out instruction is present in the message and logs the send attempt to your audit sheet.

The guardrail workflow ID is read from the GUARDRAIL_WORKFLOW_ID n8n Variable (project → Variables tab). Set it to the numeric ID from the Bar-Compliance Guardrail workflow URL: .../workflow/WORKFLOW_ID.

Returns: { approved, message_out, recipient, suppression_reason } plus all upstream fields.

nodes.name: If Approved for No-Show Text
nodes.notes:
Routes based on the compliance guardrail's decision.

Yes (approved) → sends the SMS.
No (opted out) → message suppressed; guardrail has logged the suppression.

nodes.name: Skip — opted out (no-show)
nodes.notes: Client has opted out — no-show reschedule text not sent.

nodes.name: Send Reschedule Text
nodes.notes:
Sends a gentle reschedule nudge 15 minutes after the scheduled consult.

The message reads: 'If anything came up, no worries — reschedule here: [link]'. The neutral phrasing is deliberate — this fires regardless of whether the prospect attended, so a firm tone would damage the firm's relationship with clients who did show up.

nodes.name: Send Reschedule Email
nodes.notes:
Sends the reschedule follow-up email 15 minutes after the scheduled consult.

The subject 'We Missed You' is accurate for genuine no-shows. For prospects who did attend, it reads as a minor curiosity rather than an accusation — acceptable for the free template. The done-for-you upgrade checks attendance status before sending so this only goes to actual no-shows.

parameters.subject: =We Missed You — Reschedule Your {{ $('Set Up Messages').item.json.consult_type }} with {{ $('Set Up Messages').item.json.firm_name }}
parameters.text:
=Hi {{ $('Set Up Messages').item.json.first_name || 'there' }},

We had your {{ $('Set Up Messages').item.json.consult_type }} scheduled today at {{ $('Set Up Messages').item.json.apt_time_str }} with {{ $('Set Up Messages').item.json.firm_name }}. If anything came up, no worries — life happens!

You can easily book a new time here:
{{ $('Set Up Messages').item.json.reschedule_url_final }}

We're still here to help whenever you're ready.

— {{ $('Set Up Messages').item.json.firm_name }}

---
To stop receiving text reminders, reply STOP to any of our text messages.

description: Sends confirmation, 24-hour, and 1-hour reminders (SMS + email) for every booked consult, plus a reschedule nudge 15 minutes after the scheduled start. Replaces Lawmatics sequences and paid Calendly reminder tiers. Trigger: Calendly v2 invitee.created webhook or generic booking webhook.
